title: Counting Bits
description:
For a non-negative integer `n`, build a list `ans` with `n + 1` entries, one for every integer from `0` up through `n`. Each entry `ans[i]` should hold the popcount of `i` -- that is, how many `1` bits appear in `i`'s binary representation.

**Example**

```
n = 5  ->  [0, 1, 1, 2, 1, 2]
```
starter_code:
def count_bits(n):
    # n: non-negative int. Return a list of length n + 1 with the popcount of
    # every integer from 0 to n.
    pass
hints: For any i > 0, i and i >> 1 (i with its lowest bit dropped) differ only by whether i's lowest bit is set: popcount(i) = popcount(i >> 1) + (i & 1).
Build the answer array bottom-up from ans[0] = 0, reusing already-computed smaller entries instead of recomputing popcount from scratch for each i.

solutions.name: DP via lowest-bit relation
solutions.explanation: Right-shifting i by one bit drops its least significant bit, so popcount(i) equals popcount(i >> 1) plus 1 if that dropped bit was a 1 (i.e. i is odd), or plus 0 if it was even. Since i >> 1 is always a smaller index already computed earlier in the array, this recurrence lets every entry be derived in O(1) from a previous entry, filling the whole table in one linear pass.
solutions.complexity: Time O(n), Space O(n) for the output
